response:
The fare calculation begins with a base fee of ten yuan for the first four kilometers of travel. Any distance beyond that initial four kilometers incurs an extra charge of two yuan per kilometer. This rule applies to the total driving distance which includes both actual road travel and converted waiting time. The relationship between the total fare and the distance traveled is a linear function for distances greater than four kilometers. You can determine the exact cost by adding the base fee to the product of two and the excess distance rounded up.

A passenger who paid thirty-eight yuan traveled a total calculated distance of twenty-two kilometers. Since the actual road distance was fifteen kilometers the remaining seven kilometers must represent converted waiting time. Each kilometer of converted time equals five minutes so the total stop time is thirty-five minutes. The taxi could not have stopped for more than thirty-five minutes given the fare paid. This scenario reminds us that spring brings new travelers to the city who need reliable transport. Another spring day might see even more passengers using this route between the airport and the hotel.
